texto: There is nothing more wonderful than a book. It may be a message to us from the dead, from human souls we never saw who lived perhaps thousands of miles away, and yet these little sheets of paper speak to us, arouse us, teach us, open our hearts and in turn open their hearts to us like brothers. Without books, God is silent, justice dormant, philosophy lame.

texto: Did not learned men, too, hold, till within the last twenty-five years, that a flying dragon was an impossible monster? And do we not now know that there are hundreds of them found fossil up and down the world? People call them Pterodactyles: but that is only because they are ashamed to call them flying dragons, after denying so long that flying dragons could exist.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Charles Kingsley que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Did not learned men, too, hold, till within the last twenty-five years, that a flying dragon was …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre sabedoria, belief, dinosaurs, com foco em dinosaurs e dragons.
